title: Consolidated balance sheet - 31 December 2024
summary: The Bank of England published its consolidated balance sheet as of 31 December 2024, showing total assets and liabilities of £855.1 billion, with reserves balances at £711.6 billion and loan to the Asset Purchase Facility at £659.0 billion. The balance sheet data covers five quarters of historical consolidated data on BoE assets and liabilities.
key_points: Total Liabilities and Total Assets both stood at £855,095 million as of 31 December 2024
Reserves balances (sterling liabilities) were £711,588 million, representing the largest liability component
Loan to Asset Purchase Facility was £659,015 million, the largest sterling asset
TFSME Loans totaled £102,672 million in sterling assets
Sterling denominated bond holdings were £14,842 million
One-week short-term open market operations stood at £38,075 million
why_it_matters: The BoE consolidated balance sheet provides transparency into the Bank's financial position and its exposure to various facilities including the Asset Purchase Facility and Term Funding Scheme. This data is relevant for assessing the scale of the BoE's balance sheet operations and potential future QT (quantitative tightening) considerations. The £659 billion APF loan represents the primary transmission mechanism of monetary policy through asset purchases.
macro_context: The balance sheet data reflects the BoE's monetary policy implementation as of end-2024. Reserves balances at £711.6 billion indicate the scale of commercial bank reserves held at the central bank. The APF holding of £659 billion represents the legacy stock of government bond purchases from QE programs.
market_structure_context: The balance sheet data shows the composition of BoE sterling assets including short-term open market operations (£38.1 billion one-week reverse repos), indexed long-term repo (£7.4 billion), and sterling bond holdings (£14.8 billion). Foreign currency reserve assets stood at £15.98 billion.
notes: Historical balance sheet snapshot published with significant lag. Source is factual tabular data with no commentary or analysis. Relevant for balance sheet composition analysis but provides limited actionable information for market timing or policy signals.
